title: Stacks & Queues Combined
analogy: Think of stacks and queues as two different checkout systems — a stack is like a Pringles can (LIFO: last chip in is first chip out), while a queue is like a cafeteria line (FIFO: first in, first served).

overview.summary: Stacks (LIFO) and Queues (FIFO) are the two fundamental restricted-access data structures. Understanding both and knowing when to use which is key.
learningObjectives: Compare LIFO vs FIFO access patterns.
Implement one using the other (stack from queues, queue from stacks).
Apply monotonic stacks and deques to optimize problems.
overview.interviewRelevance: Implementing one ADT using the other is a classic interview question. Monotonic stacks appear in next-greater-element problems.

paragraphs: Your browser's back button is a stack (LIFO). A print queue is a queue (FIFO). Together they model most sequential processing patterns.
Monotonic stacks and deques power sliding-window maximum and stock span problems used in finance.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: Stack = most recently added first. Queue = longest-waiting first. The choice depends on whether recency or fairness matters.

definition.technicalDefinition: A stack supports push (add to top) and pop (remove from top) in LIFO order. A queue supports enqueue (add to back) and dequeue (remove from front) in FIFO order.

vocabulary.definition: Last In, First Out — the stack access pattern.
First In, First Out — the queue access pattern.
Double-ended queue supporting push/pop from both ends in O(1).
A stack that maintains elements in non-increasing or non-decreasing order.

keyProperties: All basic operations (push, pop, enqueue, dequeue) are O(1).
A stack from 2 queues: enqueue new item, rotate all old items behind it.
A queue from 2 stacks: push to 'in' stack, pop by pouring to 'out' stack when needed.

operations.name: Queue from Two Stacks
operations.description: Use an 'in' stack for push and an 'out' stack for pop. Pour from in to out only when out is empty.
operations.visualNote: Items pour from one cylinder to another, reversing their order.

practice.title: Implement Queue using Stacks
practice.keyInsight: Two stacks: push to in-stack, pour to out-stack on pop.

practice.title: Implement Stack using Queues
practice.keyInsight: After push, rotate all previous elements behind the new one.

practice.title: Daily Temperatures
practice.keyInsight: Monotonic decreasing stack tracks unresolved days.

practice.title: Sliding Window Maximum
practice.keyInsight: Monotonic deque maintains window max candidates.

practice.title: Next Greater Element I
practice.keyInsight: Monotonic stack processes elements right to left.

conceptQuiz.question: What is the amortized time complexity of queue operations using two stacks?
conceptQuiz.explanation: Each element is moved from in-stack to out-stack at most once, so amortized cost per operation is O(1).

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: Design a data structure that supports push, pop, peek, and empty operations following FIFO order, using only LIFO stacks.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: This is the classic 'implement queue using stacks' problem.
